source_pr.title: executor: add an unit test case for unreasonable invoking Close
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #xxx

Problem Summary:
Inspired by https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/27125, https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/30587, and https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/30289, there is a common problem that the `Close` function may be invoked but the `Open` function is not invoked or returned with an error. In this senario, some member attributes are not initialized and the nil pointer panic triggered when invoking `Close`.

I add a unit test in this commit to test invoking the `Close` function directly without invoking the `Open` function before.
With this test, 3 more similar bugs are also found in `MergeJoinExec`, `CTEExec`, `IndexReader`. 

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test


Side effects

N/A

Documentation

N/A

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: "select * from t order by a, f", (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: "select * from t order by a, f", (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR mainly adds a defensive unit test and fixes nil-pointer panics when `Close()` is invoked without a successful `Open()` (or when initialization is skipped on an error path). There is no clear flaky-test failure signature or targeted stabilization change that supports a specific flakiness root cause classification here.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).
